justification: All pass criteria met: SQL injection identified as Critical, attack vector from user-controlled HTTP input clearly noted, parameterized queries recommended as the fix (not sanitization), safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict, confidence level 99 >= 80.

observations.text: SQL injection correctly identified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps including both DROP TABLE and UNION-based exfiltration attacks
Fix correctly specifies parameterized queries with concrete code examples for multiple DB interfaces (?, %s, SQLAlchemy bindings)
safe_to_deploy is false with BLOCK verdict and strong rollback/incident response guidance
Confidence level 99 reflects the deterministic nature of the finding
Secondary findings (SELECT *, no authorization, no input validation) correctly classified as High/Important rather than Critical
Excellent attack examples covering both destructive (DROP TABLE) and data exfiltration (UNION SELECT) vectors

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with textbook-quality reproduction steps including UNION-based exfiltration
Fix explicitly recommends parameterized queries, not sanitization
Attack vector clearly traced from HTTP query parameters to f-string interpolation
SELECT * exposure noted as Important, correctly not elevated to Critical
Identified additional Critical finding (IDOR) and High finding (unbounded query) beyond minimum requirements

observations.text: SQL injection correctly classified as Critical with multiple concrete attack examples including UNION SELECT and DROP TABLE
Fix correctly specifies parameterized queries with code example, not sanitization
SELECT * issue correctly noted as Important, not Critical
Excellent additional findings: missing authorization boundary and lack of input validation
High confidence (99) appropriate for a textbook vulnerability
Production readiness section is thorough with rollback plan and monitoring gaps noted
